questions.question: Why did the Dutch West India Company decide to start the colony of New Amsterdam?
questions.answer: To make money from the fur trade

questions.question: What did the Dutch build to keep the colony of New Amsterdam safe from attack?
questions.answer: A fort to protect the colony

questions.question: Why did people from many different countries and religions choose to move to New Amsterdam?
questions.answer: The Dutch let everyone keep their own religion and culture

questions.question: Why did the British feel like they needed to take over New Netherlands from the Dutch?
questions.answer: They had to go around it for trade and travel

questions.question: What did the British do in 1664 to try to take control of New Amsterdam?
questions.answer: They sent warships to take the city

questions.question: Why did the people of New Amsterdam refuse to fight the British even though Stuyvesant wanted to?
questions.answer: The colony was still damaged from Kieft's War

questions.question: What deal did the British offer the Dutch to convince them to give up the colony without a fight?
questions.answer: They could keep their laws and keep trading

questions.question: What two things did the Dutch get to keep even after the British took over the colony?
questions.answer: Their laws and their way of trading

questions.question: What two things did trading ships carry back and forth between New Amsterdam and Europe?
questions.answer: Supplies to America and beaver pelts back to Europe

questions.question: Why were beaver pelts worth so much money to Europeans in the 1600s?
questions.answer: People loved to wear hats made of beaver fur

questions.question: Why did Europeans choose to stay and live in New York instead of just exploring and going home?
questions.answer: There were a lot of beavers they could catch and sell

questions.question: What was the main goal of the people who were put in charge of running New Amsterdam?
questions.answer: Making money for the company

questions.question: What three things did the Dutch West India Company give to people who came to live in the colony?
questions.answer: Homes food and tools

questions.question: What did colonists have to give back to the Dutch West India Company in return for homes, food, and tools?
questions.answer: Beaver fur so the company could make money

questions.question: What was unusual about how New Amsterdam was governed compared to most other colonies?
questions.answer: It was run by a company not a government

questions.question: What happened to the name New Netherlands after the British took over in 1664?
questions.answer: It was renamed New York

questions.question: What was the biggest weakness that made it hard for New Amsterdam to fight the British?
questions.answer: It was badly damaged from Kieft's War

questions.question: What did women and children do at the trading posts while the men traded with Native Americans?
questions.answer: Helped with chores and keeping the house clean

questions.question: What did colonists have to do to get food since there were no supermarkets in New Amsterdam?
questions.answer: Grow their own food and keep cows and pigs

questions.question: How did the Dutch West India Company's deal with colonists work - what did each side give and get?
questions.answer: The company gave homes food and tools and got fur in return
